answers.value: Ethan tested positive for COVID-19 in late November 2020 after a known exposure on his club soccer team. His main symptoms were low-grade fever for about three days (maximum around 100.8°F), mild cough, nasal congestion, temporary loss of taste and smell, and feeling unusually tired. He did not experience any chest pain, difficulty breathing at rest, or episodes of fainting, and his oxygen saturation stayed normal at home when checked with a pulse oximeter his parents borrowed from his uncle. He recovered fully at home and never needed to go to the emergency room or be hospitalized. His pediatrician followed up with a telehealth visit and then an in-person exam before clearing him to gradually return to sports. The doctor did not feel that special heart or lung tests were necessary because his symptoms were mild, his lungs sounded clear, and his heart exam and vital signs were normal. Ethan began light jogging and basic soccer drills about two weeks after his symptoms resolved and slowly increased intensity over the next couple of weeks. By January 2021 he was back to full participation in practices and games without any lingering issues such as shortness of breath, chest discomfort, or unusual fatigue. He has not had any further COVID-19 infections or known exposures in the past 3 months. Ethan was vaccinated with the Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine as soon as he became eligible in 2021. He received his first dose on 2021-06-12 and his second dose on 2021-07-03 at a local community vaccination clinic run through the county health department. He had a sore arm for about two days after each shot and felt a bit tired and achy after the second dose, but these side effects went away quickly. He has not had any allergic reactions, chest pain, or other concerning symptoms related to the vaccine, and his pediatrician is aware of his vaccination dates and has them recorded in his chart.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form
